name: German Wirehaired Pointer
description: Developed in Germany in the late 1800s to create the ultimate versatile all-weather hunting dog, the German Wirehaired Pointer's coarse, weather-resistant wiry coat was purpose-designed to protect it from thorns, cold water, and harsh terrain. Slightly more assertive and strong-willed than its Shorthaired cousin, the GWP bonds deeply with its primary handler while remaining aloof with strangers. Their intense natural hunting drive and high daily exercise requirement make them best suited to experienced, active owners with access to open country.

conditions_detail.name: Hip Dysplasia
conditions_detail.description: Hip dysplasia is a genetic condition where the hip joint doesn't develop properly, causing the ball and socket to not fit together correctly. This leads to joint deterioration, pain, and arthritis over time.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Treatment ranges from weight management and physical therapy for mild cases to total hip replacement for severe cases. NSAIDs and joint supplements help manage pain.
prevention_tips: Screen breeding stock with hip scoring

conditions_detail.name: Entropion
conditions_detail.description: Entropion is an inward rolling of the eyelid margin, causing the eyelashes and skin to rub against the corneal surface. This produces chronic irritation, pain, corneal ulceration, and scarring that can impair vision.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Temporary eyelid tacking can relieve discomfort in puppies pending maturity. Definitive surgical correction (Hotz-Celsus procedure) is highly effective in adults.
prevention_tips: Avoid breeding dogs with severe conformational entropion
Do not breed surgically corrected dogs
